domain: Bedouin majlis (tent hospitality circle; arrival-order + tribal-status dual-axis seating; 3-cup El Dheyf/El Heyl/El Keyf coffee ritual; cup-wobble signals closure; 3-day guest law)
specific_mechanism: Dual-axis attention mask combining causal-position with status-priority gating; per-token status embedding from separate classifier; lower-status tokens cannot influence higher-status tokens.
llm_application: MAJLIS-GATE: a dual-axis attention-mask design combining (a) causal-position mask (j ≤ i, standard causal) AND (b) status-priority mask (status[j] ≥ status[i] for inclusion). Each token receives a STATUS embedding at ingestion from a separate classifier probe with categories {system, trusted-tool-output, user, untrusted-web}. Attention weights are zeroed where EITHER axis is violated. Tight status-inequality at boundaries (e.g., user tokens cannot influence system tokens at any layer, but trusted-tool-output can influence user-output tokens). Differs from Instruction Hierarchy 2404.13208 (privilege levels but not per-token attention mask), Many-Tier 2604.09443 (tier hierarchy but not status-axis mask at attention), EvoSparse (token-importance not status-class) by combining (a) DUAL-AXIS attention mask, (b) per-token classifier-derived status embedding, (c) tight status-inequality boundary at attention.
novelty_claim: Instruction Hierarchy + Many-Tier Hierarchy + EvoSparse already cover status-/priority-/importance-based attention modulation. MAJLIS-GATE recombines per-token classifier status embedding + dual-axis attention mask. Recombination of existing components.
